name: Hamida bint Muslim
title: Orphan of Karbala
shortDescription: The young daughter of Muslim ibn Aqeel who heard the news of her father's martyrdom from Imam Hussain (AS) on the way to Kufa and was taken captive on Ashura.
fullStory:
Hamida bint Muslim ibn Aqeel was the young daughter of Imam Hussain's (AS) loyal ambassador. She was present in the Karbala caravan along with her mother (Ruqayya bint Ali).

When the caravan stopped at the 'Zubala' station on the way to Kufa, the painful news of Muslim ibn Aqeel's martyrdom reached Imam Hussain (AS). Imam Hussain (AS) went to the women's tent, embraced the young Hamida, and affectionately stroked her head.

From the Imam's behavior, Hamida realized she had been orphaned and said crying: 'Has my father been killed?' Imam Hussain (AS) said with tearful eyes: 'My daughter, I am in place of your father, and my daughters are in place of your sisters.'

On the day of Ashura, Hamida witnessed the martyrdom of all her loved ones, including Imam Hussain (AS). After the tents were looted, this orphaned girl was taken captive along with the other women and children, spending the entire route from Kufa to Sham in suffering and sorrow.
quoteArabic: يا ابْنَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ! أَقُتِلَ أَبِي؟...
quote: (When the Imam stroked her head:) O son of the Messenger of God! Has my father Muslim been killed?
